ddls:
CREATE TABLE "interrupts" (
  "interrupt_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interrupt configuration */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the interrupt (e.g., R_GEN_CALL) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the interrupt functionality */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the interrupt (e.g., RO for Read-Only) */,
  "reset_value" TEXT /* Reset value of the interrupt when initialized */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level for interrupt processing */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device associated with the interrupt */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the interrupt configuration */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the interrupt configuration was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interrupt_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupts_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupt_statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status record */,
  "interrupt_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interrupt being monitored */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the interrupt is currently active (1) or inactive (0) */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last check for this interrupt status */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device associated with the status */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the status */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the status was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_statuses_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_statuses_interrupt_id FOREIGN KEY ("interrupt_id") REFERENCES interrupts ("interrupt_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupt_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "interrupt_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interrupt related to the log entry */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message detailing the log event or status */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device associated with the log entry */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the log message (e.g., INFO, WARN, ERROR) */,
  "logged_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who logged the message */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_logs_logged_by FOREIGN KEY ("logged_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_logs_interrupt_id FOREIGN KEY ("interrupt_id") REFERENCES interrupts ("interrupt_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_history" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration change record */,
  "interrupt_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interrupt whose configuration was changed */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Previous value of the configuration before the change */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the configuration after the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the change was made */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device associated with the configuration change */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the configuration change */,
  "change_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the configuration change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_history_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_history_interrupt_id FOREIGN KEY ("interrupt_id") REFERENCES interrupts ("interrupt_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, technician, admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
